key_points: The particulate model explains properties of matter using particle motion, spacing, and attraction
Solids have closely packed particles with strong attraction and limited movement
Liquids have moderate interparticle attraction allowing particles to slide past each other
Gases have very weak attraction and large spacing allowing free movement
Temperature increases particle kinetic energy causing faster motion and faster diffusion
examples: Rapid spreading of incense smoke shows fast diffusion in gases
Faster diffusion of potassium permanganate in warm water demonstrates temperature effect
Compression of air in a syringe illustrates large interparticle spaces in gases
Evaporation of water from wet clothes demonstrates particle escape from liquid surface
Boiling of water shows rapid particle motion overcoming attractive forces
formulas_or_processes: Diffusion: Rate increases with temperature due to increased particle kinetic energy
Melting: Heat energy weakens interparticle forces causing solid → liquid
Boiling: Liquid particles gain sufficient energy to escape as gas
Evaporation: High-energy surface particles escape from liquid below boiling point
Sublimation: Solid → Gas directly without becoming liquid
exam_tips: Explain properties of states of matter using particle arrangement and attraction
Relate diffusion speed to particle kinetic energy and temperature
Use scientific reasoning when explaining experiments
Remember the relationship between interparticle force and state of matter
Support answers with particle diagrams whenever possible
